title: [lichen_l2.c:548-549] lichen_link_rx called multiple times for same frame
description:
What: peer_try_all_pubkeys() iterates through all peers and calls lichen_link_rx() for each one to try signature verification. This means lichen_link_rx() is called N times for each incoming frame, where N is the number of active peers.

Why it matters: This is the intended design (documented in comment at line 475), but has performance implications:
1. Each call to lichen_link_rx() performs SCHC decompression
2. Each call performs MIC verification (AES-CCM or CRC32)
3. Only signature verification differs between iterations

The performance impact is bounded by CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS (max 64). At LoRa speeds, this overhead is acceptable, but it's not optimal.

Alternative design: Parse frame once, extract signature, try pubkeys against signature only. This would reduce redundant work.

Marking as code smell/optimization opportunity rather than bug.
close_reason: Added inline BY DESIGN comment at line 548 explaining that repeated lichen_link_rx calls are intentional for peer signature verification. The comment references the issue ID and explains the performance tradeoff (redundant SCHC/MIC work acceptable given LoRa's low packet rate and bounded peer count).
